growth_records.medium: NMS2 medium with 2.5% (vol/vol) CH4 balanced with air (methane-limited chemostat)

up.method: calculated: O2/CH4 ratio 1.11 x CH4 uptake 9.02 = 10.01

sec.method: CO2 produced (measured by off-gas GC) but per-gDW rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: He et al. Quantifying Methane and Methanol Metabolism of "Methylotuvimicrobium buryatense" 5GB1C under Substrate Limitation. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00748-19. [PMC6906744]
growth_records.snippet: Methane 0.109 ± 0.003 (h−1); Substrate uptake rate 9.02 ± 0.14 (mmol/g/h); Formate production rate 0.036 ± 0.002; OD600 2.42; Biomass yield 0.75 g/g; CCE 48%; O2/CH4 ratio 1.11 ± 0.00
growth_records.notes: Table 1, methane chemostat at D=0.109 h-1. At chemostat steady state µ=D=0.109 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/D=0.6931/0.109=6.36 h (paper cites ~6.9 h for nominal 0.1). qCH4=9.02 mmol/gDW/h. qO2 calculated from reported O2/CH4 molar ratio 1.11 x 9.02 = 10.01 mmol/gDW/h. qFormate=0.036 mmol/gDW/h. Yxs=0.75 g biomass/g methane, CCE=48%. NMS2 full salt composition by reference (3) only, so medium_composition_stated=false. T=30°C, pH 8.8 (NaOH), agitation 1000 rpm.

growth_records.medium: NMS2 medium with 2.5% (vol/vol) CH4 balanced with air (methane-limited chemostat)

up.method: calculated: O2/CH4 ratio 1.14 x CH4 uptake 5.18 = 5.91

sec.method: CO2 produced (off-gas GC) but per-gDW rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: He et al. Quantifying Methane and Methanol Metabolism of "Methylotuvimicrobium buryatense" 5GB1C under Substrate Limitation. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00748-19. [PMC6906744]
growth_records.snippet: Methane 0.054 ± 0.002 (h−1); Substrate uptake rate 5.18 ± 0.10 (mmol/g/h); Formate production rate 0.005 ± 0.001; OD600 4.55; Biomass yield 0.65 g/g; CCE 42%; O2/CH4 ratio 1.14 ± 0.00
growth_records.notes: Table 1, methane chemostat at D=0.054 h-1. µ=D=0.054 h-1 at steady state. doubling_time=ln2/0.054=12.84 h (paper cites ~13.9 h for nominal 0.05). qCH4=5.18 mmol/gDW/h. qO2=O2/CH4 ratio 1.14 x 5.18 = 5.91 mmol/gDW/h. qFormate=0.005. Yxs=0.65 g/g, CCE=42%. OD600 4.55 (~2x that at D=0.109 because gas feed constant, dilution halved). NMS2 composition by reference only.

growth_records.medium: NMS2 medium with 1 g/liter methanol (methanol-limited chemostat)

up.method: calculated: O2/CH3OH ratio 0.60 x methanol uptake 6.65 = 3.99

sec.method: CO2 produced but often below GC detection limit (~0.12% vol/vol); per-gDW rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: He et al. Quantifying Methane and Methanol Metabolism of "Methylotuvimicrobium buryatense" 5GB1C under Substrate Limitation. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00748-19. [PMC6906744]
growth_records.snippet: Methanol 0.098 ± 0.003 (h−1); Substrate uptake rate 6.65 ± 0.16 (mmol/g/h); Formate production rate 0.28 ± 0.05; OD600 2.42; Biomass yield 0.42 g/g; CCE 54%; O2/CH3OH ratio 0.60 ± 0.01
growth_records.notes: Table 1, methanol chemostat at D=0.098 h-1. µ=D=0.098 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.098=7.07 h. qMethanol=6.65 mmol/gDW/h. qO2=O2/CH3OH ratio 0.60 x 6.65 = 3.99 mmol/gDW/h (all O2 used for oxidative phosphorylation, no monooxygenase step). qFormate=0.28 mmol/gDW/h (~7x higher than on methane). Yxs=0.42 g/g, CCE=54%. NMS2 composition by reference only.

growth_records.medium: NMS2 medium with 1 g/liter methanol (methanol-limited chemostat)

up.method: calculated: O2/CH3OH ratio 0.56 x methanol uptake 3.64 = 2.04

sec.method: CO2 produced but often below GC detection limit; per-gDW rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: He et al. Quantifying Methane and Methanol Metabolism of "Methylotuvimicrobium buryatense" 5GB1C under Substrate Limitation. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00748-19. [PMC6906744]
growth_records.snippet: Methanol 0.053 ± 0.002 (h−1); Substrate uptake rate 3.64 ± 0.13 (mmol/g/h); Formate production rate 0.19 ± 0.07; OD600 2.37; Biomass yield 0.43 g/g; CCE 55%; O2/CH3OH ratio 0.56 ± 0.01
growth_records.notes: Table 1, methanol chemostat at D=0.053 h-1. µ=D=0.053 h-1. doubling_time=ln2/0.053=13.08 h. qMethanol=3.64 mmol/gDW/h. qO2=O2/CH3OH ratio 0.56 x 3.64 = 2.04 mmol/gDW/h. qFormate=0.19 mmol/gDW/h. Yxs=0.43 g/g, CCE=55%. OD600 (2.37) independent of dilution rate for liquid substrate. NMS2 composition by reference only.

up.method: reported; specific uptake at 200 ppm methane, V=dq/dTDW slope (batch). Whole-cell Michaelis-Menten Vmax(app)=18.9 +/- 0.9 mmol/gDW/h, KM(app)=8.8 +/- 1.7 uM

growth_records.cite: He et al. A methanotrophic bacterium to enable methane removal for climate mitigation. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2310046120. [PMC10466089]
growth_records.snippet: a strong linear correlation for M. buryatense 5GB1C between the specific growth rate and the methane concentration of the inlet gas from 200 ppm to 2,500 ppm, corresponding to specific growth rates from 0.004 to 0.07 h-1 ... methane uptake
growth_records.notes: Batch shake-flask methane titration. Specific growth rate mu determined by authors as slope of ln(TDW) vs time (mu=dln(TDW)/dt); reported range 0.004-0.07 h-1 across inlet methane 200-2500 ppm. Doubling time computed td=ln2/mu: at mu=0.004 -> td=0.693/0.004=173 h; at mu=0.07 -> td=0.693/0.07=9.9 h. Specific methane uptake at 200 ppm reported 0.2-0.5 mmol CH4/gDW/h (V=dq/dTDW). Whole-cell kinetics: KM(app)=8.8+/-1.7 uM (=6681+/-1291 ppm gas phase), Vmax(app)=18.9+/-0.9 mmol/gDW/h; specific affinity aos=1776+/-140 L/gDW/h. All cultivations at 30 C. NMS2 medium composition cited (refs), not restated in text -> composition:[], stated:false. pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: NMS2 (nitrate mineral salts) medium + 10 mL 1M carbonate buffer per liter

up.method: reported (1.5 +/- 0.2 mmol CH4 g-1 h-1); chemostat V=q/TDW

sec.method: calculated: text states excreted formate at 500 ppm (0.018) is roughly two times that at 1,000 ppm -> ~0.018/2=0.009 mmol/gDW/h

growth_records.cite: He et al. A methanotrophic bacterium to enable methane removal for climate mitigation. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2310046120. [PMC10466089]
growth_records.snippet: cultures grown at 500 ppm and 1,000 ppm at methane-limited steady-state in the bioreactor, with growth rates of 0.009 h-1 and 0.02 h-1 ... methane uptake rates (0.95 +/- 0.08 mmol CH4 g-1 h-1 at 500 ppm and 1.5 +/- 0.2 mmol CH4 g-1 h-1 at 1
growth_records.notes: Chemostat (methane-limited steady state) at 1,000 ppm inlet methane. At steady state mu=D=0.02 h-1 (reported). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.02=34.7 h. Specific methane uptake 1.5+/-0.2 mmol CH4/gDW/h (reported). Formate secretion: only the 500 ppm value (0.018) is given numerically; text says 500 ppm rate is roughly 2x the 1,000 ppm rate, so 1,000 ppm ~0.009 mmol formate/gDW/h (derived). 30 C, agitation 1,000 rpm. NMS2 base composition cited not restated -> stated:false. pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: NMS2 (nitrate mineral salts) medium + 10 mL 1M carbonate buffer per liter

up.method: reported (0.95 +/- 0.08 mmol CH4 g-1 h-1); chemostat V=q/TDW

sec.method: reported (0.018 +/- 0.002 mmol formate g-1 h-1) from chemostat supernatant

growth_records.cite: He et al. A methanotrophic bacterium to enable methane removal for climate mitigation. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2310046120. [PMC10466089]
growth_records.snippet: growth rates of 0.009 h-1 and 0.02 h-1 ... methane uptake rates (0.95 +/- 0.08 mmol CH4 g-1 h-1 at 500 ppm ...) ... the excreted formate rate is roughly two times higher at 500 ppm methane (0.018 +/- 0.002 mmol formate g-1 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Chemostat (methane-limited steady state) at 500 ppm inlet methane. mu=D=0.009 h-1 (reported). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.009=77.0 h. Specific methane uptake 0.95+/-0.08 mmol CH4/gDW/h (reported). Excreted formate 0.018+/-0.002 mmol/gDW/h (reported). 30 C, 1,000 rpm. NMS2 base composition cited not restated -> stated:false. pH not stated. Non-growth ATP maintenance (NG-ATPM) fit ~0.36-0.4 mmol ATP/gDW/h (context, not a flux record).
